title: [InstCombine] Drop nuw flag when CtlzOp is a sub nuw
summary: The generalization transforms a specific vector bit_ceil pattern into a robust scalar/vector optimization by abstracting the core logic. Developers identified the key components: a CTLZ with a transformed input, a shift by (bitwidth - CTLZ), and a select guarding against overflow. They extracted the condition-checking into `isSafeToRemoveBitCeilSelect`, which uses `ConstantRange` to safely validate that the CTLZ operand and select condition derive from the same value through invertible operations (like `add` or `sub`). This allows matching varied forms (e.g., `sub nsw i32 -2, %x` instead of `add i32 %x, -1`). The patch also handles NUW flags correctly—dropping them when safety requires—ensuring the transform applies to both scalar and vector types without introducing undefined behavior. The approach demonstrates how to lift a concrete test case into a reusable utility by isolating the semantic constraints and verifying them symbolically.
